title: Day 04 Quiz — useEffect, Fetch, and Async UI
description: Verify you understand promises, fetch, useEffect, dependency arrays, and async UI states before Day 05 routing.

questions.prompt: Why should you not call fetch directly in the component body during render?

options.text: fetch is not available in browsers
It triggers a side effect on every render and can cause infinite loops or race conditions
fetch only works inside useEffect on the first render

questions.explanation: Render should be pure. Side effects like network calls belong in useEffect or event handlers, not unconditional render code.

questions.prompt: An empty dependency array [] means the effect runs once after the initial mount (in Strict Mode dev you may see double invoke).
questions.explanation: [] tells React the effect does not depend on props/state values that change — typical for mount-only setup. Strict Mode intentionally double-invokes in development to surface bugs.

questions.prompt: fetch('https://api.github.com/users/missing') resolves with status 404. What should you check before calling response.json() as success?

options.text: response.ok or response.status — fetch does not reject on HTTP errors

questions.explanation: fetch resolves on HTTP error status codes like 404 — you must check response.ok or status manually.

questions.prompt: What is the purpose of useEffect in React?

options.text: Synchronize the component with external systems after render — APIs, subscriptions, DOM

questions.explanation: useEffect runs after paint to handle side effects outside the render phase.

questions.prompt: Which UI states should a search feature handle? Select all that apply.

options.text: Loading while request is in flight
Error when network fails or user not found

questions.explanation: Production API UIs always model loading, error, empty, and success — interviewers expect this matrix.

questions.prompt: Declare an async function inside useEffect or use an {{blank1}} function with {{blank2}}/{{blank3}} — never make the effect callback itself async.
questions.explanation: async effect callbacks return Promises React ignores. Use an inner async function and call it.

questions.prompt: Complete the effect that refetches when username changes.
questions.explanation: Values used inside the effect that change over time belong in the dependency array.

questions.prompt: Infinite re-renders after adding useEffect. The effect calls setState and has no dependency array. What is wrong?

options.text: Effect runs after every render; setState triggers another render — loop

questions.explanation: Missing deps means effect runs every render. If it sets state, you get render → effect → setState → render forever.

questions.prompt: Why type API responses with TypeScript interfaces?
questions.sampleAnswer: Interfaces document the expected JSON shape, catch typos at compile time, and improve autocomplete when rendering user.login or avatar_url.
questions.explanation: APIs return JSON — TypeScript does not validate runtime shape without extra tools, but types still help development enormously.

questions.prompt: User types fast and multiple fetches overlap. Older response arrives last and shows stale data. What pattern helps?

options.text: AbortController or ignore flag in cleanup to cancel/ignore stale requests

questions.explanation: Cleanup in useEffect can abort fetches or set a cancelled flag — critical for search UIs. Full debounce comes Day 06.
